text: The disorienting angle and the surreal glow of blue light create an uncanny, almost liminal feeling within me. The silence and emptiness of the office or classroom beyond the glass amplify a sense of detachment from ordinary time—am I arriving too late or too early? I wonder about the unseen people who fill and empty these spaces. This solitude is not painful, but contemplative; I am suspended between purpose and abandonment, curious about what kind of work or discovery happens here, and what it means to be present in such a place.
